business_clusters.name: Locks & Brass Hardware
business_clusters.description: Dindigul is India's lock capital — over 70% of Indian locks are manufactured here. The cluster comprises 800+ MSMEs producing 35 crore locks annually, exporting to 40+ countries.

business_clusters.name: Granite & Stone Quarrying
business_clusters.description: Rich granite deposits across Dindigul district support a thriving quarrying and export sector. Premium Black Galaxy and Tan Brown granite varieties serve global architectural markets.

business_clusters.name: Agro Processing & Dairy
business_clusters.description: Banana, coconut, groundnut, and dairy processing clusters support thousands of farmers and agro-entrepreneurs. Aavin dairy is a major employer in the district.

business_clusters.name: Textiles & Leather Tanning
business_clusters.description: Palani and Natham taluks host a significant textile weaving and leather tanning cluster supplying raw and finished leather to Ambur and Chennai exporters.

business_clusters.name: Tea & Coffee Plantations
business_clusters.description: The high-altitude Megamalai region produces premium quality orthodox tea and arabica coffee with GI-tag potential, exported to Europe and the Middle East.

business_clusters.name: Cardamom & Spices
business_clusters.description: Cumbum Valley is one of India's premier cardamom belts — producing fragrant green cardamom, pepper, and allied hill spices exported through the Spices Board.

business_clusters.name: Banana Cultivation & Export
business_clusters.description: Theni is one of Tamil Nadu's top banana growing districts. Poovan and Nendran varieties are exported to Sri Lanka, Malaysia, Singapore, and the Gulf via APEDA-registered exporters.

business_clusters.name: Eco-Tourism & Hospitality
business_clusters.description: Megamalai, Kurangani, and Suruli Falls attract eco-tourists year-round. The district's homestay and resort sector is among the fastest growing in Tamil Nadu.

places.name: Dindigul Rock Fort
places.description: A magnificent 17th-century fort atop a 90m granite outcrop. Built by the Madurai Nayaks and later held by Hyder Ali, it offers panoramic views of the city. The fort also houses a small church and mosque, symbolising religious harmony.

places.name: Sirumalai Hills
places.description: A serene hill range rising to 1,650m, blanketed with coffee estates, waterfalls, and dense shola forests. Ideal for trekking, bird-watching, and weekend getaways from Dindigul.

places.name: Palani Murugan Temple
places.description: One of the six sacred abodes of Lord Murugan — the Arulmigu Dhandayuthapani Swamy temple atop Sivagiri Hill attracts over 2 crore pilgrims annually. The idol is made of Navapashanam — nine medicinal herbs.

places.name: Kodaikanal Hill Station
places.description: The 'Princess of Hill Stations' at 2,133m. The star-shaped Kodaikanal Lake, Bryant Park, Coaker's Walk, Silver Cascade Falls, and Pillar Rocks are iconic attractions drawing 4 lakh tourists annually.
places.best_time: April to June, September to November

places.name: Kamarajar Memorial Museum
places.description: Dedicated to Perunthalaivar K. Kamarajar — born in Virudhunagar, deeply connected to Dindigul. The museum chronicles his transformative contributions to Tamil Nadu's education and industrial growth.

places.name: Megamalai Wildlife Sanctuary
places.description: A pristine high-altitude sanctuary at 1,500m bordering Kerala. Home to Asian elephants, leopards, gaurs, lion-tailed macaques, and 230+ bird species. Tea estates create a stunning mosaic landscape.

places.name: Vaigai Dam & Reservoir
places.description: A scenic earthen dam across the Vaigai River in the Western Ghats foothills. Supplies water to Madurai and Dindigul. The reservoir is a popular family picnic spot with boating facilities.

places.name: Suruli Falls
places.description: A spectacular 151-foot cascade in the Megamalai foothills, surrounded by dense forest. The Suruli Murugan Temple at the base adds spiritual significance to this natural wonder.

places.name: Kurangani Hills
places.description: A trekker's paradise at 1,840m, famous for the Kurinji (Strobilanthes kunthiana) flowers that bloom once every 12 years. The next bloom is due in 2030. Excellent viewpoints over the Theni plains.

places.name: Cumbum Valley
places.description: Known as the 'California of South India', Cumbum Valley's microclimate supports grape cultivation, cardamom plantations, and stunning valley views. The Bodi-Gudalur route offers scenic drives.
